reasoning: The Lean formalization faithfully captures all components. (1) `countably_compact X` encodes "every countable open cover has a finite subcover" by quantifying over `ℕ → Set X` — this is the standard encoding equivalent to countable collections (repetition doesn't change unions/finite subcovers). (2) `limit_point_compact_strong X` uses `ClusterPt x (𝓟 (U \ {x}))`, which by Mathlib's `accPt_principal_iff_clusterPt` lemma is literally equivalent to `AccPt x (𝓟 U)` — exactly Munkres' definition of "x is a limit point of U" (every neighborhood of x contains a point of U different from x). (3) `Infinite U` for `U : Set X` (via subtype coercion) correctly means U is an infinite set. (4) `T1Space X` matches "T_1 space", and the `↔` matches "equivalent". No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier structure matches the informal statement.
